name: Abd al-Rahman ibn Aqeel
title: Brave of Aqeel
shortDescription: One of the brave members of Aqeel's family who, after the companions' martyrdom, was among the first of Bani Hashem to fight bravely.
fullStory:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Aqeel ibn Abi Talib was one of the valiant and brave youths of the Bani Hashem family. He was the cousin of Imam Hussain (AS) and the brother of Muslim ibn Aqeel (the martyred ambassador of Kufa). The strong presence of Aqeel's family in the tragedy of Ashura demonstrates the insight and sacrifice of this family in defending Wilayah.

After all the non-Hashemite companions of Imam Hussain (AS) were martyred on the battlefield, it was the turn of the household of the Prophet (PBUH) and Amir al-Mu'minin (AS). Abd al-Rahman ibn Aqeel was one of the first who, with indescribable bravery, asked the Imam (AS) for permission to enter the field.

He entered the field with a firm posture and recited war poetry with a loud voice: 'My father is Aqeel, so know my position; I am from the lineage of Hashem, and I have brothers as firm as mountains...'. With these verses, he showcased the nobility and purity of his family to the Kufan soldiers who had surrounded the son of their Prophet for Ibn Ziyad's coins.

Despite being surrounded by thousands, Abd al-Rahman swung his sword with all his might. In this heroic battle, he brought down 17 horsemen of Umar Sa'd's army and struck fear into the hearts of the Kufans.

Finally, Uthman ibn Khalid al-Juhani and a man from the Bani Hamdan tribe cowardly attacked him from two sides and martyred him with sword strikes. His blood-soaked body was placed alongside the other martyrs of Bani Hashem as a testament to the loyalty of Aqeel's family.
quoteArabic: أَبي عَقیلٌ فَاعْرِفوا مَكاني، مِنْ هاشِمٍ وَهاشِمٌ إِخْواني
quote: My father is Aqeel, so know my position; I am from Hashem, and Bani Hashem are my brothers.
